title: caralluma lutea subsp. vansonii
intro: Caralluma lutea subsp. vansonii is a rare succulent plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The extracts of Caralluma lutea subsp. vansonii are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as weight management, improved digestion, and enhanced cardiovascular health. Caralluma lutea subsp. vansonii can be prepared into herbal preparations, including capsules, powders, and teas, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
